title: eriogonum effusum var. fendlerianum
intro: Eriogonum effusum var. fendlerianum, also known as Fendler's buckwheat or desert buckwheat, is a perennial shrub in the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae) that grows in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Fendler's buckwheat include treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Eriogonum effusum var. fendlerianum include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
